Georeferencing Report

76a Borrhålskarta med dagskarta.jpg

Executive Summary

GOOD   The georeferencing of this image is rated good.

14 ground control points were identified across an 4×4 grid on the scanned image. 2 grid positions could not be matched, typically where map content obscured the printed crosses. No outliers were detected.

The root-mean-square positional error across all control points is 0.57 m. The georeferenced output is in EPSG:3006 and is ready for use in GIS.

Control Point Overlay

The image below shows each detected control point plotted on the scanned map. Green markers indicate accepted points; red markers indicate statistical outliers.

Each control point was identified by template-matching a synthetic cross pattern against the scanned image. Positions are refined to sub-pixel accuracy using parabolic interpolation on the correlation surface.

Residual Analysis

A six-parameter affine transformation was fitted from pixel coordinates to source map coordinates. The residuals below show how well each control point fits this model. Points exceeding twice the RMS (1.136 m) are flagged as outliers.

Processing Methodology

1. Cross detection

A synthetic cross template (arm length 20 px, line thickness 3 px, scaled for 301 DPI) was matched against the greyscale image using normalised cross-correlation (cv2.matchTemplate with TM_CCOEFF_NORMED). Peaks above a correlation threshold of 0.7 were extracted via non-maximum suppression.

Each candidate was verified by analysing the pixel intensity profile: the horizontal and vertical arms must be dark, the centre must be dark, and the diagonal corners must be significantly brighter than the arms. Duplicate detections were merged, keeping the highest-correlation candidate. Positions were refined to sub-pixel accuracy using parabolic interpolation.

2. Grid organisation

Detected crosses were clustered into columns and rows using 1D gap-based clustering with an automatically determined gap threshold. The interior grid was identified by finding the longest chain of consistently spaced positions (tolerance 12%).

3. Affine-validated gap filling

A least-squares affine model was fitted from the directly detected crosses. For each empty grid cell, a local template search was performed around the affine-predicted position. A gap-filled point was accepted only if its correlation exceeded 0.65 and its position was within 15 px of the prediction.

4. Coordinate assignment

Map coordinates were assigned based on each point’s grid position using the supplied grid origin (100100.0, 78900.0) and spacing (200.0, -100.0).

5. Transformation

Coordinates were transformed from EPSG:3152 to EPSG:3006 using pyproj.Transformer.

6. Output

The final VRT file contains the transformed GCPs and can be opened directly in QGIS or processed with GDAL tools.

Tools and libraries

  • OpenCV (cv2) — template matching, non-maximum suppression
  • NumPy — array operations, least-squares fitting
  • Pillow (PIL) — image loading
  • pyproj — coordinate transformation (PROJ wrapper)
  • Matplotlib — overlay plot generation
